Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Columbia Mechanical Contractors, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Defendant's Exhibit A ( Plaintiffs' complaint). (Evidence Code section 452(d).) Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that decedent Robert Mingram was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Robert Cantley's testimony that he worked with decedent at one job site in the early 1960s versus the declaration of Valerie Howerton Miller stating that Defendant did not perform work prior to 1971 is a triable issue of fact. (Defendant's Index of Exhibits, Ex. E; Ex. F at 94:10-95:7; 95:20-96:6; 98:9-14; 192:12-25; 199:11-24.) Further, Mr. Cantley testified he worked around Defendant's guys "a hundred times." (Renken Declaration, Ex. A at 99:15-18.) Order modified to remove appearances and signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
